title: Asian Paneer Noodles (No Onion-Garlic)
tagline: Glossy Schezwan-soy noodles with crispy marinated paneer — no onion or garlic.
servingsNote: Uses spaghetti as the noodle — works perfectly for this style.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. Solid gym-meal ratio thanks to 200 g paneer (~36 g protein) on top of the wheat noodles.
Lean version: use low-fat paneer (saves ~150 kcal a serving) and cut the finishing oil/butter — the Schezwan and chilli oil carry plenty of flavour on their own.
Want more protein without more fat: push paneer to 250 g, or add a whisked egg tossed through the noodles at the end for a scramble.

items: 200 g paneer, cut into 2 cm cubes

steps.title: Marinate the paneer
steps.body: Mix 1 tsp soy sauce + 1 tsp Schezwan sauce + 1/2 tsp chilli oil + 1/4 tsp black pepper + a pinch of MSG + 1 tsp oil in a bowl. Toss the 200 g paneer cubes in it and leave 15-20 minutes. Keep the coating light, not watery — don't add salt, the soy already has it.

steps.title: Boil and cool the noodles
steps.body: Cook the 180 g spaghetti in salted water until just done (slightly under, not soft). Drain, rinse briefly under cool water to stop the cooking and stop sticking, then toss with a few drops of oil. Rinsing matters here — it keeps the noodles separate for the stir-fry.

steps.title: Sear the paneer
steps.body: Heat a pan on medium-high. Add the marinated paneer in a single layer and sear undisturbed so the edges caramelise — because of the marinade use medium-high (not max) so it sticks and browns instead of burning. Turn to get golden crisp edges all over, ~4-5 min. Remove and set aside.

steps.title: Char the capsicum
steps.body: Same pan, add 2 tbsp oil on high heat. Add the sliced capsicum (~150 g) and cook just 2-3 minutes — you want a slight char but still crunchy. Don't overcook it or the dish goes soft and dull.

steps.title: Build the sauce
steps.body: Push the capsicum to the side and add 1 tbsp Schezwan sauce + 1 tbsp soy sauce + 1-2 tsp chilli oil + 1 tsp vinegar + a pinch of sugar + a tiny pinch of MSG. Let it sizzle 10-15 seconds so it deepens.

steps.title: Toss it all together
steps.body: Add the noodles and toss hard on high heat so they coat evenly, adding a splash of water if dry. Let them sit untouched for 10-15 seconds once to catch a little char, then toss again. Add plenty of black pepper.

steps.title: Finish with paneer
steps.body: Fold the seared paneer back in at the very end and toss just to warm through — don't cook it again or you'll lose the crust. Finish with a tiny knob of butter and a few drops of chilli oil on top for aroma.

notes: No onion or garlic on purpose. The flavour comes from browning the paneer, high heat on the capsicum, soy/Schezwan umami and the vinegar acid — hit those and it doesn't taste empty.
Marinating the paneer is a high-ROI move if you have 15-20 min — it becomes a flavour carrier inside the noodles instead of just texture. Skip salt, the soy covers it.
Only have capsicum? Fine. Good technique beats more veg. But carrot, cabbage, beans or mushroom all work if you have them — cook the harder veg first.
Skip herbs like basil here — it clashes with soy and chilli. Coriander is okay if you want a garnish.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
